title: jasminum sambac var. goaense
intro: Jasminum sambac var. goaense, also known as the Goa jasmine or Chandan pushpi, is a fragrant, climbing plant belonging to the family Oleaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Jasminum sambac var. goaense include its potential to aid in reducing stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote respiratory health. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to harness its therapeutic benefits.
